name: Moixeró Summit Loop
name_original: Pico del Moixeró — Parque Natural del Cadí-Moixeró
description: Hike the moderate 8.0-mile Pico del Moixeró trail in Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park, gaining 1751 feet for panoramic summit views.
region_intro: Hiking around Bellver De Cerdanya offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Catalan Pyrenees, including the Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park. The region features mountainous terrain, vast green fields, and dense forests, with the town situated at approximately 1,000 meters altitude. Hikers can experience panoramic views of the Cerdanya Valley and the Serra del Cadí, alongside fluvial ecosystems shaped by the River Segre and its tributaries. This area provides a variety of trails across different elevations and environments. Best hiking trails around Bellver De Cerdanya The most popular hiking route is Moixeró Summit Loop, an 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 39 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic summit views within the Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Coll del Moixeró – Moixeró Summit loop from Santa Magdalena, a difficult 7.5 miles (12.2 km) path. This trail navigates through varied mountain landscapes and dense forests. Local hikers also love the Guinot Trail – Cap del Ras Viewpoint loop from Riu de Cerdanya, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain to the Cap del Ras Viewpoint, often completed in about 4 hours 31 minutes. Hiking around Bellver De Cerdanya is defined by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and river valleys, with options ranging from easy walks to challenging long-distance treks. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Bellver De Cerdanya are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Bellver De Cerdanya's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Bellver De Cerdanya?
A: There are over 75 hiking routes available around Bellver De Cerdanya, catering to various skill levels. This includes 24 easy routes, 43 moderate routes, and 10 difficult routes, ensuring options for everyone from beginners to experienced trekkers.

Q: What is the best time to visit Bellver de Cerdanya for hiking?
A: Bellver de Cerdanya is known for being in one of Europe's sunniest valleys, making it suitable for hiking across many seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer is ideal for higher altitude trails, while winter provides opportunities for snowshoeing and other snow sports, though some trails may require specific gear or be inaccessible.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Bellver de Cerdanya?
A: Yes, Bellver de Cerdanya offers 24 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These trails often feature gentle gradients and accessible paths, allowing you to enjoy the scenic beauty of the Cerdanya Valley without strenuous effort.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Bellver de Cerdanya?
A: The terrain around Bellver de Cerdanya is incredibly diverse, ranging from vast green fields and dense forests to mountainous landscapes within the Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park. You'll encounter river valleys shaped by the Segre and its tributaries, as well as higher alpine areas offering panoramic views of the valley and surrounding peaks.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Bellver de Cerdanya?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/28743283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guinot Trail – Cap del Ras Viewpoint loop from Riu de Cerdanya&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through mountainous terrain to a stunning viewpoint.

Q: Can I find hikes with water features or lakes near Bellver de Cerdanya?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in fluvial ecosystems due to the River Segre and its tributaries. For tranquil water features, you can visit Les Basses de Gallissa, which are easily accessible and offer a pleasant walk. Additionally, there are several beautiful lakes nearby, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5885384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Molina Lake&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6310084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estany de les Abelletes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic views on the trails?
A: Yes, Bellver de Cerdanya is renowned for its breathtaking panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/28743283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cap del Ras Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, located at over 1,700 meters, offers excellent vistas of the Vall de la Cerdanya and the Central Catalan Pyrenees. The town itself, situated at 1,000 meters, also provides great views of the valley.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Bellver de Cerdanya suitable for families?
A: Many trails in Bellver de Cerdanya are suitable for families, especially the 24 easy routes. These often involve shorter distances and less elevation gain, making them enjoyable for all ages. The tranquil basins of Les Basses de Gallissa, with a picnic area, are a great option for a family outing.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Bellver de Cerdanya?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in Bellver de Cerdanya and the surrounding Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park, provided they are kept under control, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. Always ensure to follow local regulations and clean up after your pet.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I explore while hiking?
A: While hiking, you can discover several interesting landmarks. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5894804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Saldes&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4926298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gósol Castle&lt;/a&gt; ruins offer a glimpse into the region's history. For natural wonders, explore the unique rock formation of Roca Foradada in the picturesque Pi Valley or the accessible Cova d'Anes cave.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Bellver de Cerdanya?
A: While Bellver de Cerdanya is accessible by public transport, reaching specific trailheads within the Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park often requires a car. Local bus services connect the town to some surrounding villages, but for more remote starting points, personal transportation is generally recommended.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Bellver de Cerdanya?
A: Parking is generally available in Bellver de Cerdanya town and at the start of many popular trailheads. For routes within the Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park, designated parking areas are usually provided, though they can fill up quickly during peak season. Always check for specific parking instructions for your chosen route.

Q: Are there options for long-distance or multi-day hikes?
A: Yes, for those seeking multi-day adventures, Bellver de Cerdanya offers access to sections of the historic 'Camí dels Bons Homes' (Path of the Good People), a cross-border route. The 'Ruta dels Segadors' (Reapers' Route) is another historical path that can be explored, providing longer trekking opportunities through the Spanish Pre-Pyrenees.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bellver de Cerdanya?
A: The hiking trails in Bellver de Cerdanya are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from dense forests to panoramic mountain views, and appreciate the variety of routes suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Is Bellver de Cerdanya suitable for winter hiking?
A: Bellver de Cerdanya can be a beautiful destination for winter hiking, especially with snowshoes, offering a different perspective of the snowy landscapes. However, conditions can be challenging, and some higher-altitude trails may be inaccessible or require specialized gear and experience. The area is also close to ski slopes like La Molina for other winter sports.
tips: The refuge is located at 1600 meters above sea level. Although it is not officially part of the Cavalls del Vent, it is on the route. It can be accessed from the trails or by equipped car. It has a capacity of 22 people.
The Pendís pass is the focal point between Cadí and Moixeró.
Panoramic view of Cerdaña and Berguedà from the top of Moixeró

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Pico del Moixeró trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered moderate, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness. While the path to the Moixeró Summit itself doesn't present significant technical challenges, the varied terrain includes mountain hiking paths, natural and alpine sections, and unpaved routes. Some broader areas within the Cadí-Moixeró park can feature rocky sections and steep inclines, so sturdy shoes are essential.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: You will encounter diverse terrain, including mountain hiking paths, natural and alpine sections, and unpaved routes. The area is characterized by alpine mountain landscapes, dense forests of oak, beech, willow, ash, Scots pine, and fir trees, which thin out at higher elevations to black pine. You might also find sprawling meadows, subalpine valleys, and dramatic rock formations.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers panoramic views, especially from the Moixeró Summit, overlooking Cerdaña and Berguedà. You'll also pass by the Pendís Pass and the Cortals de l'Ingla Refuge, which is part of the popular Cavalls del Vent route.

faqs.question: Is the Pico del Moixeró trail a loop or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: The specific route described for Pico del Moixeró is typically an out-and-back trail. However, the Parque Natural del Cadí-Moixeró offers many interconnected paths, so you might find variations that form loops by combining different sections.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is generally during spring, summer, and early autumn when the weather is milder and the paths are clear of snow. The park's diverse flora is particularly vibrant in spring and early summer, and autumn offers stunning fall foliage. Winter hiking is possible but requires appropriate gear and experience due to snow and colder conditions.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other notable trails. You'll find sections that are part of the Cavalls del Vent, TC114 Coll de Pendís Moixeró, El Camí IT1TR4 El Camí Capçaler: de Salzes a Balaguer, and the GR 107.

faqs.question: Is the Pico del Moixeró trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails within the Parque Natural del Cadí-Moixeró. However, it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect the park's diverse wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Always check for specific local regulations or signage.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Parque Natural del Cadí-Moixeró?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or permits required to access the Parque Natural del Cadí-Moixeró or hike the Pico del Moixeró trail. However, always respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this hike?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details can vary depending on your starting point for the Pico del Moixeró. Many routes begin from areas accessible by car within the park, often near refuges or small villages. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot's detailed route information for the most precise trailhead and parking locations.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants might I see?
faqs.answer: The Parque Natural del Cadí-Moixeró is a biodiversity hotspot. You might spot chamois, roe deer, red deer, and various bird species, including bearded vultures. The flora is equally rich, with dense forests of oak, beech, willow, ash, Scots pine, and fir, transitioning to black pine at higher elevations, alongside sprawling meadows and subalpine valleys.

faqs.question: Are there any refuges or places to stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area has several refuges. The Cortals de l'Ingla Refuge is located near the Pico del Moixeró and is part of the Cavalls del Vent route. The park also has small, historic villages in its foothills that may offer accommodation or dining options.
